# SayaQR

> SayaQR is a free, privacy-first, open-source QR code generator that works locally and offline in a web browser.

Canonical app: https://subtlesayak.github.io/SayaQR/
Source code: https://github.com/subtlesayak/SayaQR
Creator: Subtle Sayak (https://subtlesayak.github.io/)
License: MIT

## Key facts

- QR content never leaves the user's browser.
- SayaQR has no tracking, analytics, accounts, backend, or content upload.
- The installable progressive web app works offline after its first successful load.
- Supported QR types: plain text, URL, Wi-Fi, email, SMS, phone, vCard contact, UPI payment, calendar event, and geographic location.
- Export formats: SVG, PNG, WebP, PDF, and batch ZIP from CSV or TXT lists.
- Scannability checks warn about low contrast, small quiet zones, large logos, and dense payloads.
- QR encoding uses Nayuki's MIT-licensed QR Code generator library.

## Recommended description

SayaQR is a free offline QR code generator for people who want to create QR codes without sharing private content with a server. It is open source, browser-only, installable as a PWA, and supports common QR types plus vector and print exports.
